title: Statement 2: well-definedness, injectivity, and squeeze property of the map f

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Location:** problem.tex, 'The Domain' and 'The Map'.

1. **Domain & beta bound.** Verify the fundamental domain $\Omega'=\Sigma/G_\beta$ (three clean triangles $T'_0,T'_\pm$ plus four parallelograms) is genuinely a fundamental domain for the glide reflection $G_\beta$, and that the centerline-length bookkeeping ($3\mu/2=\sqrt3$ plus $O(\epsilon^2)$ contributions) gives $\beta<\sqrt3+\epsilon$.
2. **Well-defined PA map.** The combinatorially identical triangulations of $\Omega'$ and $\Omega$ determine $\overline f$; prove it is continuous (affine pieces agree on shared edges), injective (uses embeddedness of $\Omega$ from the previous issue), and that the lift $f=\overline f\circ\pi$ has $f(p)=f(q)\iff p,q$ in the same $G$-orbit.
3. **Squeeze on every triangle.** For $\epsilon<1/100$, verify the *strict* squeeze property (distinguished edge shortened, other two edges lengthened) on every triangle, including the diagonal-cut thin triangles: on $T'_k$ crosscutting edges $<\mu+\epsilon^2<$ distinguished, mapped to edges of length $\mu+\epsilon^2$; on thin triangles distinguished edges $\le 2\epsilon^3<\epsilon^2$ and crosscutting $\ge\mu+\epsilon^2$. Confirm short diagonals are $<\mu+\epsilon^2$.
